content.problemStatement:
Chef and Chefina are at positions XXX and YYY on a number line.

They both love badminton.

It is known that badminton courts are located at every integer point.

They want to find a court such that the maximum distance travelled by either of them is **minimized**.

Formally, suppose they choose the badminton court at position ZZZ. You need to find the minimum value of max⁡(∣X−Z∣,∣Y−Z∣)\max(|X-Z|, |Y-Z|)max(∣X−Z∣,∣Y−Z∣) across all possible choices of ZZZ. Here, ∣X∣|X|∣X∣ denotes absolute value of XXX.

Report this minimum value.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two space-separated integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum possible value of max⁡(∣X−Z∣,∣Y−Z∣)\max(|X-Z|, |Y-Z|)max(∣X−Z∣,∣Y−Z∣).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y (X ≠ Y), the positions of Chef and Chefina on the number line.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the minimum possible value of max(|X‑Z|, |Y‑Z|) over all integer court positions Z.

solution.approach: The value max(|X‑Z|,|Y‑Z|) is minimized when Z is as close as possible to the midpoint of X and Y. The optimal radius is the smallest integer r such that an integer Z exists with both |X‑Z| ≤ r and |Y‑Z| ≤ r. This radius equals ceil(|X‑Y| / 2). Hence for each test case compute d = |X‑Y| and answer = (d + 1) / 2 using integer division.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it)); Y = int(next(it))
        d = abs(X - Y)
        out.append(str((d + 1) // 2))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
